evidence_quotes.quote: preserve the right for customers to keep the games they bought.
Companies actively delete access to already digitally bought games after the support ends for these games.

paragraphs.text: Dear Sir or Madam, I would like to raise awareness on a initiative called "Stop Killing Games". This movement wants to preserve the right for customers to keep the games they bought. Companies actively delete access to already digitally bought games after the support ends for these games. There are lists of games out there that have already been "erased" and cannot be legally accessed any longer. This clearly violates our European customer laws. Games, physically or digitally, should not be taken away from customers after purchase at any point in time. These purchases must be respected and access in any shape or form must be given to customers. There has to be a end of support plan by companies for these scenarios. Please consider this in the Digital Fairness Act. Perhaps at a later stage this topic can be discussed for Digital Ownership. Many Thanks
